slots.label: Rubber insert durability
slots.summary: A verified review reports complete deterioration and structural failure ('literally falling apart') of the rubber insert near the ear within two years of long-term wear — the only negative signal for this aspect, with no mitigating or explanatory information found in product documentation or imagery.

slots.label: Optical accuracy
slots.summary: Reported optical inaccuracy during walking or navigating terrain indicates possible lens distortion or misalignment; no supporting technical specs, certifications, or corrective guidance (e.g., fit adjustments) were retrieved.

slots.label: Rubber insert durability

slots.label: Optical accuracy
slots.summary: A review (sunglasses_010_review_0064) reports optical inaccuracy specifically when walking or navigating terrain, but no product documentation, test data, or image-based evidence (e.g., distortion visuals) was retrieved to substantiate or explain this issue.

slots.label: Add explicit durability specifications for rubber components (e.g., 'tested for 2+ years of daily wear', 'UV- and sweat-resistant thermoplastic rubber') to product description and packaging.
slots.summary: Rubber insert failure is the only critical durability complaint, cited in a long-term wear context, and lacks any manufacturer durability claims or visual verification in current assets.; Failure mode ('falling apart') implies material or design flaw with potential safety/retention implications.

slots.label: Include third-party optical certification (e.g., ISO 14889 compliance) and add a brief usage note: 'Optimized for static and moderate-motion outdoor use; not recommended for high-speed cycling or trail running.'
slots.summary: Optical accuracy is flagged in a functional use case (walking/navigation) but unsupported by evidence; unverified claims may erode trust if repeated.; Category 4 lenses + polarization should theoretically support visual clarity—but real-world performance depends on lens geometry, base curve, and optical grade.

slots.label: Surface the exact listed price and any active promotions (e.g., 'Now $49.99 — Save $20') in primary product metadata and image overlays (e.g., badge on hero image).
slots.summary: No numeric price or discount indicators were retrieved despite a positive price sentiment — limiting ability to validate value proposition or benchmark competitively.; Pricing transparency directly impacts conversion and reduces post-purchase dissonance.

slots.label: Publish annotated macro images of rubber inserts (front/side/ear interface) with captions highlighting material composition and stress-test results.
slots.summary: Rubber insert degradation was claimed but not visually confirmed in available images; adding close-up, labeled imagery of earsocks (pre- and post-wear simulation) would preempt doubt and demonstrate quality control.; Image-based evidence is highly trusted for material claims.
